author_claims.claim_text: CSF drainage/perfusion protocols are repeatedly used as spinal cord protection measures, but benefits must be balanced against drain complications and prophylactic-versus-therapeutic timing.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for exact estimates and protocol details.

author_claims.claim_text: Staged repair and collateral-network strategies are plausible conditioning approaches, but registry and meta-analysis data require approach-specific interpretation.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for exact estimates and protocol details.

author_claims.claim_text: Direct/local ischemic preconditioning and postconditioning show stronger spinal-cord-specific animal protection than remote conditioning.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for exact estimates and protocol details.

author_claims.claim_text: Remote ischemic conditioning evidence is not yet equivalent to direct spinal cord protection unless spinal cord endpoints are measured.
author_claims.notes: First-pass claim abstraction; full text needed for exact estimates and protocol details.

author_claims.claim_text: Anesthetic, hypothermic, and pharmacologic protection signals are model- and bundle-dependent and should not be assigned causal weight without timing and comparator details.

author_claims.claim_text: Neuromonitoring can guide rescue and risk detection but is not itself proof of protection unless linked to intervention and endpoint recovery.

boundary_records.boundary_statement: CSF drainage evidence must separate prophylactic, therapeutic, rescue, and complication endpoints.
Staged repair evidence must preserve open/endovascular/hybrid approach and extent-of-coverage boundaries.
Direct spinal cord ischemic conditioning and remote conditioning cannot be pooled without endpoint directness labels.
Animal aortic-occlusion protection signals require species, occlusion duration, reperfusion timing, and histology/behavior boundaries.
Pharmacologic, anesthetic, hypothermic, and perfusion bundles require attribution boundaries because multiple maneuvers are often combined.
Neuromonitoring evidence must separate detection/risk stratification from causal neuroprotection.
Clinical trial registry rows must remain status/protocol evidence until results are linked.
Perioperative ischemia prevention should not be generalized to chronic traumatic SCI reconstruction without mechanism and timing boundaries.

consensus_drafts.draft_statement: CSF drainage and perfusion-pressure protection is consensus-ready as a first-pass perioperative ischemia-protection statement, but full-text extraction is needed before quantitative effect-size or protocol-strength claims.
